perturbation.original_text: In this Agreement, the following words and phrases shall have the following meanings: (a) "Affiliate" shall have the same meaning as contained in the Canada Business Corporations Act; "Company Affiliate" shall mean any Affiliate of the Company; (b) "Company Property" means any and all information, equipment, hardware, components, documents and other property of the Company or any Company Affiliate provided to or used by Turpin, including all computers, monitors, laptops, personal digital assistants, mobile computing devices, computer peripherals, cell phones and other telephones, pagers, storage media, security cards, keys, calling cards, charge cards, reference materials, designs, specifications, schematics, drawings, diagrams, pictures, notes, memoranda, papers, manuals, records, and the like
perturbation.changed_text: In this Agreement, the following words and phrases shall have the following meanings: (a) "Affiliate" shall have the same meaning as contained in the Canada Business Corporations Act; "Company Affiliate" shall mean any Affiliate of the Company that has a direct reporting relationship to the Company's CEO; (b) "Company Property" means only the specifically enumerated list of hardware provided directly to Turpin by the Company: a single laptop and a cell phone. No other items or information, regardless of their origin or usage, shall be considered Company Property under this definition
perturbation.explanation: The definition of "Company Affiliate" is narrowed to include only those with direct reporting to the CEO, conflicting with the broader definition under the Canada Business Corporations Act as initially stated. The definition of “Company Property” is now narrowed to only include a specific laptop and a cellphone. This contradicts the later section where the return of all Company Property is required upon termination, as other items used or created may not be considered Company Property under this definition, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Turpin shall deliver to the Company all Work Product and Company Property, including all originals and copies thereof, in Turpin's possession and/or control, at the request of the Company, or, in the absence of such a request, upon the termination of Turpin's employment with the Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of 'Company Property' to only a laptop and cellphone, creating a direct contradiction with the contradicted text that requires 'all Work Product and Company Property' to be returned. This alteration directly conflicts with the initial definition, leading to ambiguity and potentially making the original obligation unenforceable. The change follows the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction, as the terms are inconsistently defined, making enforcement difficult or interpretation unclear.

perturbation.original_text: Work Product. Turpin agrees to maintain at all times adequate and current records relating to the creation and development of the Work Product and Intellectual Property Rights therein, which records and all copies thereof shall be and shall remain the exclusive property of the Company, and to disclose all such records and copies to the Company promptly.
perturbation.changed_text: Work Product. Turpin agrees to maintain records relating to the creation and development of the Work Product and Intellectual Property Rights therein only when explicitly requested by the Company in writing, and only those explicitly requested records and their copies shall be considered the property of the Company. Disclosure of such records will only occur if legally mandated.
perturbation.explanation: The modification changes the obligation of Turpin to maintain and disclose records related to the Work Product. The original text states an unconditional obligation to maintain adequate records at all times, whereas the modified text restricts this obligation only when explicitly requested by the Company in writing, and disclosure is contingent on legal mandates. This creates an in-text contradiction because in another section states that Company is the owner of Work Product, but the definition added now, creates ambiguity on who controls them and maintains them.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Turpin acknowledges and agrees that all Work Product have been made for the Company and that the Company shall be the exclusive owner of all right, title, and interest in and to the Work Product and all Intellectual Property Rights therein. Turpin does hereby assign and transfer to the Company, effective upon creation, all right, title, and interest that Turpin may have in and to the Work Product and all Intellectual Property Rights therein and does hereby assign all of Turpin's future right, title, and interest that Turpin may have in and to each of the Work Product and Intellectual Property Rights therein, effective at the time each is created. Turpin hereby irrevocably and expressly waives as against any person all moral rights Turpin may have in any and all Work Product.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates Turpin to maintain and disclose records of the Work Product and Intellectual Property Rights at all times, establishing the Company's immediate access and ownership. The changed text restricts Turpin's obligation to maintain records only when explicitly requested by the Company in writing and limits disclosure to legally mandated instances. This directly contradicts the "Contradicted Text", which states that the Company shall be the exclusive owner of all right, title, and interest in and to the Work Product and all Intellectual Property Rights. The change introduces ambiguity and weakens the company's ownership and access rights, creating a strong in-text contradiction as it pertains to control and ownership of the Work Product. This is an Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction, as the obligations are now unclear.

perturbation.original_text: Turpin shall, at the Company's request, assist with, execute and deliver all further documents, applications, declarations, verifications, submissions, transfers and assignments and do all other things requested by the Company, acting reasonably, during the term hereof and thereafter, at the expense of the Company, but without additional compensation, to enable the Company or its nominees to apply for, acquire, prosecute, perfect, enforce and/or maintain any and all right, title and interest, in any country, in and to the Confidential Information, the Work Product and the Intellectual Property Rights in same.
perturbation.changed_text: Turpin may, at Turpin's convenience, choose to assist with documents, applications, etc., if the Company provides reasonable compensation for the time spent. This assistance is not guaranteed and depends on Turpin's current availability and priorities, and is not required after the termination of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates Turpin to assist with legal processes at the Company's request, without additional compensation. The modified text introduces an element of choice for Turpin, contingent on compensation and availability, and removes the obligation after termination. This change contradicts the earlier section which assigns all rights in the Work Product to the company with an unconditional obligation, whereas the added modification, diminishes these rights and creates uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Turpin acknowledges and agrees that all Work Product have been made for the Company and that the Company shall be the exclusive owner of all right, title, and interest in and to the Work Product and all Intellectual Property Rights therein. Turpin does hereby assign and transfer to the Company, effective upon creation, all right, title, and interest that Turpin may have in and to the Work Product and all Intellectual Property Rights therein and does hereby assign all of Turpin's future right, title, and interest that Turpin may have in and to each of the Work Product and Intellectual Property Rights therein, effective at the time each is created. Turpin hereby irrevocably and expressly waives as against any person all moral rights Turpin may have in any and all Work Product.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates Turpin to assist with legal processes at the Company's request, without additional compensation. The modified text introduces an element of choice for Turpin, contingent on compensation and availability, and removes the obligation after termination. This creates a direct contradiction with the original agreement where Turpin acknowledges and agrees that all Work Product have been made for the Company and that the Company shall be the exclusive owner of all right, title, and interest. The change directly conflicts with the original agreement and introduces ambiguity and inconsistencies, which aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.
